data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: Limits the time it takes a user to make a decision by reducing the number of choices presented.
data.technical_spec: Maintains a clean, minimalistic interface with a single search bar focus.

data.title: Fitts's Law
data.description: Prioritizes ease of access to frequent functions through intuitive UI layout.
data.technical_spec: The search bar is prominently placed and highly accessible as the central feature.

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Encourages user engagement through visually pleasing, yet functionally simple designs.
data.technical_spec: Simplicity in design increases perceived usability, leading to higher user satisfaction.

data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Adapts design elements common in other sites to match user's existing mental models.
data.technical_spec: Consistency in search layout and results aligns with user expectations from other web experiences.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: Limits cognitive load by organizing information into smaller chunks for easier comprehension.
data.technical_spec: Search results are concise, often displaying just enough information to reduce the choice paradox.
